explanations.2: anyio is a library that lets you run multiple async tasks at the same time in a way that works with different async backends.
explanations.13: Annotated is a type hint wrapper that lets you attach extra metadata (like Doc and DefaultPath) to a type without changing what the type actually is.
explanations.31: The await keyword waits for this chain of container operations to finish; the chain ends with stdout() which returns the text output from running the commands.
explanations.63: This creates a persistent storage volume that survives between container runs, so mypy can reuse cached type information and run faster the next time.
explanations.74: The f-string (f"/app/config/mypy.ini") lets you embed the variable value directly into the string.
explanations.98: This creates an async task group so all four methods can run in parallel instead of waiting for each one to finish before starting the next.
